steps.title: Translate 6% growth into a multiplier
steps.content: Growth by 6% means multiplying by (100% + 6%) = 106% = 1.06 each month. Do not use 0.06 (which represents keeping only 6%, not adding 6%) or 1.6 (which represents 60% growth).

steps.title: Write the exponential equation
steps.content:
Exponential growth repeats the same multiplier each period. After $m$ months of applying the multiplier 1.06:
$$c = 1,500 \times (1.06)^m$$
That matches Choice B — $c=1,500{ \left(1.06\right)}^{m}$.
